items.text:
Mailroom Notice: Updated Package Pickup Procedure — Effective March 1

Beginning March 1, all student packages will be processed through the new automated locker system in the lobby of Wilson Hall. The previous walk-up counter service in Wilson Hall basement will be discontinued. Students will receive an email notification and a six-digit pickup code as soon as their package arrives at the mailroom.

Please review the following changes:
- Lockers may be accessed 24 hours daily using your student ID and code
- Packages must be picked up within 5 days, or a $3 daily late fee will apply
- Oversized items (over 50 pounds) still require staff handling at the new Service Desk, Room 110
- The Service Desk is open Monday through Friday, 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM

For questions about your delivery, please contact mailroom@campus.edu or visit the Service Desk during open hours. The new system is expected to reduce wait times during peak delivery periods such as the start of each semester.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the penalty for late package pickup?

options.A: A return shipment back to the original sender immediately.
options.B: A weekly storage fine of fifteen dollars total per week.
options.C: A flat handling charge of five dollars per delivery.
options.D: A daily fee of three dollars after five days.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'a $3 daily late fee will apply' if packages are not picked up within 5 days.

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the notice about the basement counter service?

options.A: It will continue alongside the new locker system.
options.B: It will no longer handle student package pickups.
options.C: It will be moved to the Wilson Hall lobby.
options.D: It will only operate during evening hours.

questions.explanation: The notice says the previous walk-up counter service 'will be discontinued,' implying it will no longer handle pickups.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To remind students about late-fee policies for delayed packages here.
options.B: To advertise the new oversized-package handling service desk.
options.C: To announce a change in how packages are picked up.
options.D: To list contact details for the campus mailroom office.

questions.explanation: The notice introduces a new automated locker system and explains the procedure change, with details and fees in support of that announcement.

items.text:
Subject: Friday's titration lab — quick coordination

Hi Aaron,

I wanted to check in before Friday's titration experiment in Bromley 224. Here is what we need to sort out ahead of time.

For supplies, I've already booked us a workstation for the 2:00 PM section. We will need to bring our own safety goggles since the department is out of loaners this week. Dr. Patel mentioned the stockroom should reopen by next Tuesday.

For the procedure itself, I can handle the burette setup and recording if you want to focus on the calculations and writing up the results. The lab report is due Sunday by 11:59 PM through the chemistry portal.

One thing — could you arrive by 1:45 PM? Last time we lost ten minutes finding the right reagent shelf, and the late penalty for incomplete reports is $5.

Let me know if any of this doesn't work for you.

Thanks,
Maya

questions.stem: According to the email, what time has Maya booked the workstation for?

options.A: The 2:00 PM section of the lab.
options.B: The 1:45 PM time slot before lunch.
options.C: The 11:59 PM session on the portal.
options.D: The Tuesday session of next week.

questions.explanation: Maya writes that she 'already booked us a workstation for the 2:00 PM section.'

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Aaron and Maya bring to the lab themselves?

options.A: Their own copy of the lab report instructions.
options.B: Their own safety goggles for the experiment.
options.C: Their own burette and titration setup tools.
options.D: Their own backup lab partner just in case.

questions.explanation: The email states 'we will need to bring our own safety goggles since the department is out of loaners.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the email about Aaron's punctuality?

options.A: He has never been late to any chemistry lab session.
options.B: He typically arrives early on every Friday morning.
options.C: He has been late to a previous lab session.
options.D: He always finishes lab reports before the deadline.

questions.explanation: Maya's request to arrive by 1:45 PM and her note that 'last time we lost ten minutes' implies Aaron had been late before.
